topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

My phone buzzed with a notification, the screen lighting up my darkened room. A profile picture flashed: a familiar, sneering grin I hadn't seen in over a decade. It was Mark. Mark, the architect of my awkward middle school years, the one whose shadow I spent most of my formative years trying to escape. My breath hitched. My fingers froze above the screen, the glass suddenly feeling slick and cold under my touch. Who on earth would send this? A sudden knot tightened in my stomach, pulling my shoulders up toward my ears. I quickly minimized the message, my heart beginning a frantic, uneven rhythm against my ribs. I stared at the screen, rereading the username, a wave of intense unease washing over me.

[story 2]

The chime of a new connection pulled me out of a deep dive into a book. I glanced at the notification, and the world seemed to tilt slightly. It was a request. Mark. The name hit me like a physical blow. My hands started trembling, the tremor spreading up my arms. I dropped the book onto the floor with a soft thud. I leaned closer to the screen, my gaze locked on the profile picture—that arrogant smirk that used to make my skin crawl. A sudden, sharp intake of air escaped my lungs. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small space between my bed and the window, my movements jerky and uncontrolled. Every instinct screamed at me to delete it, to ignore it, but I couldn't move.

[story 3]

I was scrolling mindlessly through social media when the notification popped up. It was a friend request. My eyes scanned the sender's name, and a cold wash spread through my chest. It was him. The boy who had perfected the art of making me feel small. A strange tightness constricted my throat, making it difficult to draw a full breath. I gripped the edge of my desk until my knuckles turned white. My gaze darted back and forth between the request and the door, listening intently for any sound that wasn't my own pulse hammering in my ears. A profound sense of wrongness settled over me, heavy and suffocating.
